mnemonics: S.A.M.P.L.E - Scheduling Across Multiple Processors Looks Expensive
keyPoints: Load balancing is required to distribute work evenly across processors.
Processor affinity allows a process to prefer running on the same processor it ran on before to exploit cached data.
Symmetric multiprocessing (SMP) is a common architecture where each processor is self-scheduling from a common ready queue.
commonMistakes: Assuming all scheduling algorithms designed for single CPUs work optimally for multiple processors without modification.
Ignoring the significant performance impact of cache misses when a process migrates to a different processor.
